perturbation.original_text: The CONTRACTOR must neither assert copyrights or o ther rights on documents, information, knowledge and data based on documents, information, knowledge and data which is put down to the PURCHASER, nor make or hav e made objects for himself or third parties, in which or upon the manufacture of which documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER directly or indir ectly are used, unless the PURCHASER has given written approval explicitly bef orehand.
perturbation.changed_text: The CONSULTANT must neither assert copyrights or o ther rights on documents, information, knowledge and data based on documents, information, knowledge and data which is put down to the PURCHASER, nor make or hav e made objects for himself or third parties, in which or upon the manufacture of which documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER directly or indir ectly are used, unless the PURCHASER has given written approval explicitly bef orehand.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CONTRACTOR' to 'CONSULTANT' in Section IV introduces another term without defining it. This further undermines the defined relationship between the PURCHASER and the CONTRACTOR. This section outlines the rights and obligations of the CONTRACTOR, but by changing it to CONSULTANT, it creates uncertainty about whether the original CONTRACTOR is still bound by these provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONTRACTOR must make all adequate arrangements in his premises, where he carries out work for the PURCHASER, in order to adh ere to the non-disclosure agreement according to this agreement; he will especially ens ure that third parties will not gain access to the locations where the PURCHASER orders are han dled.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'CONTRACTOR' whose obligations are clearly defined elsewhere in the contract. By changing 'CONTRACTOR' to 'CONSULTANT' in the changed text, it creates ambiguity as to which entity is bound by the obligations outlined in the contradicted text, which specifically refers to 'CONTRACTOR'. The change undermines the defined relationship, leading to uncertainty and a potential conflict between which term should be enforced, based on the definition for Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The CONTRACTOR must make all adequate arrangements in his premises, where he carries out work for the PURCHASER, in order to adh ere to the non-disclosure agreement according to this agreement; he will especially ens ure that third parties will not gain access to the locations where the PURCHASER orders are han dled.
perturbation.changed_text: The PROVIDER must make all adequate arrangements in his premises, where he carries out work for the PURCHASER, in order to adh ere to the non-disclosure agreement according to this agreement; he will especially ens ure that third parties will not gain access to the locations where the PURCHASER orders are han dled.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'CONTRACTOR' to 'PROVIDER' in Section V, we are adding to the already established confusion, we are adding yet another undifined term to the text. This section requires the CONTRACTOR to make adequate arrangement, however it the term is being changed into PROVIDER, an undifined term, that obligation might not be enforced, since it creates doubts on the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONTRACTOR is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form as far as confidential documents, info rmation, knowledge, samples and data are made available to these in the course of the co operation or they can gain access to documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER. On request, Seite 2/3 the CONTRACTOR will present the non-disclosure agre ement to the PURCHASER according to clause 1.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'CONTRACTOR' which is a defined term. The changed text replaces 'CONTRACTOR' with 'PROVIDER', an undefined term, to refer to the same entity obligated to make arrangements. The contradicted text relies on the term 'CONTRACTOR' to impose secrecy obligations. By changing 'CONTRACTOR' to 'PROVIDER' in the changed text, it creates a ambiguity on who is responsible of that obligation, contradicting the requirement of the 'CONTRACTOR' in the contradicted text to commit employees to secrecy, as the term is not clearly defined.

perturbation.original_text: The CONTRACTOR is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form as far as confidential documents, info rmation, knowledge, samples and data are made available to these in the course of the co operation or they can gain access to documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER. On request, Seite 2/3 the CONTRACTOR will present the non-disclosure agre ement to the PURCHASER according to clause 1.
perturbation.changed_text: The VENDOR is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form as far as confidential documents, info rmation, knowledge, samples and data are made available to these in the course of the co operation or they can gain access to documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER. On request, Seite 2/3 the CONTRACTOR will present the non-disclosure agre ement to the PURCHASER according to clause 1.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'CONTRACTOR' to 'VENDOR' in Section VI while keeping the term CONTRACTOR after, further erodes the document's clarity. It is contradictory in the same location. The first part of the section VI is reffering to the VENDOR, while the last sentence is reffering to the CONTRACTOR.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The VENDOR is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form as far as confidential documents, info rmation, knowledge, samples and data are made available to these in the course of the co operation or they can gain access to documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER. On request, Seite 2/3 the CONTRACTOR will present the non-disclosure agre ement to the PURCHASER according to clause 1.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'CONTRACTOR' to 'VENDOR' in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction. The first part of the section refers to 'VENDOR', while the last sentence refers to 'CONTRACTOR'. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency within the same section of the document, because in the same clause both parties are mentioned. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The CONTRACTOR will observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are obliged to data secrecy in terms of § 5 BDSG.
perturbation.changed_text: The CONTRACTOR will observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the SUPPLIER is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are obliged to data secrecy in terms of § 5 BDSG.
perturbation.explanation: The substitution of 'CONTRACTOR' with 'SUPPLIER', in this section. It is contradictory in the same location. The first part of the section X is reffering to the CONTRACTOR, while the second part is reffering to the SUPPLIER.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONTRACTOR will observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the SUPPLIER is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are obliged to data secrecy in terms of § 5 BDSG.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CONTRACTOR' with 'SUPPLIER' in a section that previously referred consistently to the 'CONTRACTOR'. This creates a contradiction because the section now refers to both the 'CONTRACTOR' and the 'SUPPLIER', making it unclear who is responsible for the obligations outlined. The explanation states that the contradiction occurs because the first part of the section refers to the CONTRACTOR, while the second part refers to the SUPPLIER, contradicting itself in the same location. The contradiction is in-text and impactful because it directly changes who is accountable under the contract, leading to a strong contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, especially considering the contradiction occurs within the same sentence.
